title: Dead Dogs and Gym Teachers

jokes.text: SURE, LINDSAY. YOU CAN GO SEE THE WHO. AND YOU CAN GO SEE THE ROLLING STONES AT ALTAMONT, TOO.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asks to go to The Who concert
jokes.punchline: Dad sarcastically references the infamous Altamont concert where someone died
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural reference that shows dad's knowledge while being overprotective. Good economy and character-specific humor.

jokes.text: WHAT'S NOT FUN ABOUT SITTING AROUND IN A CLOUD OF MARIJUANA SMOKE, WATCHING SOME MANIAC WAG HIS BLOODY TONGUE?
jokes.punchline: Vivid, over-the-top description of rock concerts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of parental paranoia with vivid imagery. Character-appropriate overreaction.

jokes.text: THAT'S NOT THE WHO, DAD. THAT'S KISS.
jokes.punchline: Lindsay matter-of-factly corrects him
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan correction. Great economy and shows Lindsay knows more than dad assumes.

jokes.text: THE WHO SMASH THEIR GUITARS.
jokes.setup: Explaining The Who aren't that bad
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits they destroy instruments
jokes.explanation: Good comedic timing - trying to defend them then immediately contradicting herself.

jokes.text: WE TOOK HER TO SEE RICH LITTLE LAST SUMMER.
jokes.setup: Parents think Lindsay has been to concerts before
jokes.punchline: Rich Little impressionist show as 'concert' experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect generational disconnect humor. Shows parents' square sensibilities vs. rock culture.

jokes.text: [IMITATING JOHNNY CARSON] MAY AN OVERFED YAK RELIEVE HIMSELF ON YOUR BUICK.
jokes.punchline: Absurd Carson curse about yaks and Buicks
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd and specific joke. The yak/Buick combination is memorably weird.

jokes.text: MOM, RICH LITTLE ISN'T A CONCERT.
jokes.setup: Parents think Rich Little counts as concert experience
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's exasperated correction
jokes.explanation: Good straight-person reaction to parents' misunderstanding of what concerts are.

jokes.text: WELL, YOU TELL THAT TO THE 2,000 PEOPLE THAT WERE LAUGHING THEIR BUTTS OFF.
jokes.setup: Lindsay says Rich Little isn't a concert
jokes.punchline: Mom defends it with audience size
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing mom's defensiveness about their entertainment choices.

jokes.text: SAYS THE GUINNESS BOOK OF WORLD RECORDS THAT THEY'RE THE LOUDEST BAND ON EARTH.
jokes.punchline: Sam drops specific factual knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good geek moment for Sam, showing his encyclopedic knowledge in unexpected areas.

jokes.text: HEY, IF I'M NOT ALLOWED TO SEE KENTUCKY FRIED MOVIE EVER, THEN YOU SHOULDN'T BE ALLOWED TO SEE THE WHO.
jokes.punchline: Sam's logical but petty comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling logic and great character moment. Shows Sam's sense of fairness.

jokes.text: I'LL BE LISTENING TO IT BACKWARDS, TOO.
jokes.setup: Dad agrees to listen to The Who album
jokes.punchline: References moral panic about backwards messages
jokes.explanation: Excellent period-specific humor about 1980s moral panic. Perfect character moment showing dad's paranoia.

jokes.text: NICE SHOT, HAVERCHUCK.
jokes.punchline: Coach's sarcastic 'compliment'
jokes.explanation: Standard gym teacher sarcasm. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: TOLD YOU NOT TO PASS IT TO HIM.
jokes.punchline: Neal's 'I told you so' moment
jokes.explanation: Typical friend dynamics. Shows Neal's slightly mean streak.

jokes.text: YEAH, A MAGIC SCHOOL BUS.
jokes.punchline: Kim connects it to children's educational show
jokes.explanation: Good callback to kids' show that deflates Daniel's cool reference. Kim's dry wit shines.

jokes.text: YOU CAN'T EVEN PLAY THE GUITAR, THANK GOD.
jokes.setup: Daniel wants to write Lindsay a song
jokes.punchline: Nick's relief that Daniel can't play
jokes.explanation: Good friend dynamics - Nick knows Daniel's romantic gestures would be terrible.

jokes.text: MICHAEL, ROW THE BOAT ASHORE HALLELUJAH
jokes.setup: Daniel claims to be a guitar master like Pete Townshend
jokes.punchline: Plays children's folk song instead of rock
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectations. Perfect character moment showing Daniel's delusions vs. reality.

jokes.text: THIS ISN'T THE CAFETERIA FROM FAME.
jokes.punchline: Kim's cutting pop culture reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim moment - cutting down pretension with sharp cultural reference.

jokes.text: WHO THE HELL'S COCO?
jokes.punchline: Daniel doesn't get the reference
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Daniel's confusion. Shows different cultural knowledge.

jokes.text: SHE WAS THAT HOT CHICK WHO TOOK HER SHIRT OFF IN THE MOVIE.
jokes.punchline: Nick reduces character to sexualized moment
jokes.explanation: Shows Nick's teenage male perspective. Character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: OH...DEAD.
jokes.punchline: Single word describing terrible day
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and delivery. Shows working-class exhaustion in one word.

jokes.text: I MADE 18 BUCKS IN TIPS TODAY. HOW IS ANYBODY SUPPOSED TO LIVE ON THAT, HUH?
jokes.punchline: Specific low dollar amount emphasizes struggle
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing economic reality. Specific number makes it real.

jokes.text: OH, NO, YOU'RE NOT GONNA START DANCING AGAIN, ARE YOU?
jokes.punchline: Bill's worried about previous embarrassing job
jokes.explanation: Great setup for embarrassing revelation. Shows Bill's awareness of family's financial desperation.

jokes.text: BESIDES, THEY DON'T WANT AN OLD BROAD LIKE ME ANYMORE.
jokes.punchline: Gloria's blunt assessment of her market value
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about aging and sex work. Character's matter-of-fact delivery sells it.

jokes.text: MY GYM TEACHER?!
jokes.setup: Mom reveals she's dating Mr. Fredricks
jokes.punchline: Bill's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment. Bill's horror at the intersection of home and school life.

jokes.text: PHYSICAL EDUCATION TEACHER.
jokes.punchline: Gloria's attempt at dignity through job title
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gloria trying to elevate the relationship's respectability.

jokes.text: I HEARD STEVIE NICKS IS A WITCH.
jokes.punchline: Casual statement about witchcraft
jokes.explanation: Good period-specific teen rumor. Shows how myths spread among teenagers.

jokes.text: SHE DOES WITCH STUFF. SHE CASTS SPELLS ON PEOPLE.
jokes.setup: Kim asks what she means about witch
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's matter-of-fact explanation of 'witch stuff'
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Lindsay believes teenage gossip. Technical term 'witch stuff' is funny.

jokes.text: CUT OFF A LOCK OF DANIEL'S HAIR AND STICK IT TO A VOODOO DOLL. THAT WAY, WHENEVER HE GETS OUT OF LINE, I'LL JUST JAB HIM WITH A PIN, AND HE'LL SHUT UP.
jokes.punchline: Kim's specific voodoo plan for relationship control
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim moment - practical approach to supernatural boyfriend management. Dark and funny.

jokes.text: THE WORLD WILL SURVIVE WITH ONE LESS SQUIRREL.
jokes.setup: Lindsay wants to check if they hit something
jokes.punchline: Kim's callous dismissal of potential roadkill
jokes.explanation: Classic Kim callousness. Sets up the dog tragedy with smaller animal death joke.

jokes.text: ROCKY II. MUCH BETTER THAN THE FIRST ONE.
jokes.punchline: Declares sequel better than original
jokes.explanation: Shows Fredricks' lowbrow tastes. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: BILL MURRAY'S THE FUNNIEST MAN ON THE PLANET.
jokes.punchline: Bill's passionate defense
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bill's comedy appreciation vs. Fredricks' conventional views.

jokes.text: BILL MURRAY'S THE FUNNIEST MAN ON THE PLANET.
jokes.punchline: Bill's passionate defense sets up conflict
jokes.explanation: Bill's comedy appreciation creates immediate tension with potential stepfather

jokes.text: WE DON'T HAVE A RESTROOM. WE HAVE A TOILET.
jokes.punchline: Correcting euphemism with blunt truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill moment - deflating pretension with working-class directness.

jokes.text: [SNORTS] WHO'S GOLIATH?
jokes.punchline: Kim's callous snort and question
jokes.explanation: Shows Kim's lack of empathy. The snort sells her callousness.

jokes.text: LIKE, WITH YOUR BARE HANDS?
jokes.setup: Lindsay says she killed Millie's dog
jokes.punchline: Nick's assumption of violent method
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd response. Nick's stoned logic leads to ridiculous conclusion.

jokes.text: I ALWAYS SAY...GIRL PLUS CAR EQUALS DEAD ANIMAL.
jokes.punchline: Daniel's fake-wise mathematical formula
jokes.explanation: Perfect Daniel pseudo-wisdom. The mathematical format makes the sexism more absurd.

jokes.text: THEY'RE STUPID, AND THEY SMELL, AND THEY'RE UGLY. THEY DON'T EVEN LIKE US PEOPLE. THEY PRETEND TO, BUT THEY'RE JUST KISSING OUR ASSES SO WE'LL FEED 'EM. I AIN'T BUYING INTO THAT HOODWINK.
jokes.punchline: Ken's cynical anti-dog rant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ken cynicism. Takes contrarian position on universally loved animals with conspiracy theory.

jokes.text: I HEARD RADAR O'REILLY HAS, LIKE, NO FINGERS. THAT'S WHY HE ALWAYS CARRIES A CLIPBOARD.
jokes.punchline: Neal's conspiracy theory about actor's disability
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd theory. Shows how teens create explanations for random details.

jokes.text: I'LL SEE YOU IN GYM CLASS, BIG GUY.
jokes.setup: Fredricks being overly familiar with Bill
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at bonding
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Fredricks trying too hard to connect with Bill.

jokes.text: WHAT THE HELL WAS THAT? WHAT--I--WH-WHAT? HE ATE YOUR MOM'S POT ROAST?
jokes.setup: Fredricks mentions eating at Bill's house
jokes.punchline: Sam's shocked realization about the relationship
jokes.explanation: Sam's dawning horror at the domestic implications

jokes.text: BOBO. HE WAS SO SMART. HE USED TO SIT WITH ME AND WATCH TV ALL DAY LONG. HE LOVED TV.
jokes.setup: Lindsay making up story about dead dog
jokes.punchline: Specific details about dog's TV viewing habits
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's kind attempt to connect, even through fabrication.

jokes.text: ALL OF A SUDDEN, HE WOULD START TO FROTH AT THE MOUTH, AND THEN HIS LITTLE LEGS WOULD GET ALL TENSE, AND HE WOULD START SHAKING AND THEN... POOP HIMSELF. AND GET HIS PAWS IN THE POOP AND GET POOP ALL OVER THE PLACE.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly graphic and uncomfortable details
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Lindsay's fabricated story gets too detailed and gross.

jokes.text: SHE IS KINDA HOT.
jokes.punchline: Sam's inappropriate comment about friend's mother
jokes.explanation: Good awkward teenage moment. Sam's honesty creates uncomfortable truth.

jokes.text: I HAVE BETTER THINGS TO DO THAN RUN AROUND A GYM AND SHOOT A STUPID BALL IN A HOOP.
jokes.punchline: His dismissive description of basketball
jokes.explanation: Good Bill rebellion moment. Reductive description of sports shows his contempt.

jokes.text: PICK MY NOSE AND EAT IT...OR READ THE COMICS OR GO TO THE BATHROOM. ALL THOSE ARE BETTER THAN GYM CLASS.
jokes.setup: Asked what better things he has to do
jokes.punchline: List starting with gross behavior, ending mundane
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill moment - gross-out humor mixed with mundane activities to show his contempt.

jokes.text: FOR ONCE IN YOUR LIFE, SHUT YOUR BIG, STINKIN' TRAP, OK?
jokes.punchline: Bill tells off classmate who was encouraging punishment
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Bill's pent-up anger finding multiple targets.

jokes.text: WHAT ARE YOU GONNA DO, CALL MY MOM?
jokes.punchline: Bill's sarcastic challenge, not knowing they're dating
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony. Bill unknowingly hits on the most awkward possible threat. Perfect setup.

jokes.text: [WHISPERING] I never thought I'd say this, but Haverchuck's the King!
jokes.punchline: Sam's whispered admiration
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing even the geeks recognize Bill's courage in standing up to authority.

jokes.text: AND YOU WOULD HUMP HER FOOT...AND WE THOUGHT THAT WAS FUNNY...AND THEN YOU GOT SPAYED, AND YOU DIDN'T DO IT ANYMORE.
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to say something meaningful about dead dog
jokes.punchline: Awkward sexual memory at funeral
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - inappropriate memory shared at solemn moment.

jokes.text: EVER SINCE MY DOG DIED, THEY'VE BEEN LETTING ME DO ANYTHING.
jokes.setup: Millie going to Who concert despite parents
jokes.punchline: Exploiting parental guilt over dog death
jokes.explanation: Millie learning to manipulate grief for freedom - ironic given Lindsay's guilt

jokes.text: OH, MY GOD. I AM SO BAD.
jokes.setup: Millie decides to blow off homework
jokes.punchline: Treats minor rule-breaking as extreme rebellion
jokes.explanation: Perfect good-girl overreaction to minor misbehavior

jokes.text: MAMA'S GOT A SQUEEZE BOX, DADDY NEVER SLEEPS AT NIGHT
jokes.setup: Family listening to seemingly innocent song
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo in lyrics becomes apparent
jokes.explanation: Setup for parents' reaction to sexual content. The song itself provides the comedy.

jokes.text: 'CAUSE SHE'S PLAYING ALL NIGHT IS IT JUST ME, OR DOES THAT SOUND FILTHY?
jokes.setup: Parents listening to The Who's 'Squeeze Box'
jokes.punchline: Dad recognizes sexual innuendo, mom misses it
jokes.explanation: Dad's dawning realization about song's true meaning while mom stays innocent

jokes.text: SHE GOES IN AND OUT AND IN AND OUT AND IN AND OUT
jokes.punchline: Repetitive sexual innuendo becomes undeniable
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of sexual content that parents can't ignore.

jokes.text: 'IN AND OUT'? THE INSTRUMENT.
jokes.punchline: Parents' uncomfortable realization and weak deflection
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing parents' discomfort when they can't deny the innuendo.

jokes.text: HE WRITES LIKE A MENTAL PATIENT.
jokes.punchline: Description of Ken's handwriting
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Ken. Shows Lindsay's practical problems with befriending burnouts.

jokes.text: THAT MILLIE'S GONNA START HANGING OUT WITH US AND THEN YOU'RE NOT GONNA HAVE ANYONE TO RUN TO WHEN YOU GET SCARED OF YOUR BAD FRIENDS?
jokes.punchline: Kim's cutting insight into Lindsay's motivations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight. Kim cuts through Lindsay's rationalizations to the real fear.

jokes.text: I'M NOT SEEING THAT STRIPES CRAP AGAIN.
jokes.punchline: Fredricks' dismissal of Bill's favorite comedy
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing continued conflict between Bill and Fredricks' tastes.

jokes.text: REALLY? I LOVE GO-CART CITY.
jokes.setup: Gloria suggests taking Bill to go-carts
jokes.punchline: Fredricks' enthusiastic response
jokes.explanation: Shows Fredricks' childlike enthusiasm. Reveals his attempts to connect aren't entirely calculated.

jokes.text: HE'S FAT. AND HE'S NOT EVEN IN GOOD SHAPE. AND HE NEVER RUNS, YOU NEVER SEE HIM PLAY THE GAMES WITH US.
jokes.setup: Bill listing complaints about gym teacher
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Fredricks doesn't meet his own standards
jokes.explanation: Good observation about hypocrisy. Bill's logical critique of authority figure.

jokes.text: 'WE'? UH...OK. ALL RIGHT. WE CAN ALL GO.
jokes.setup: Neal invites himself and Sam on Bill's outing
jokes.punchline: Fredricks' uncomfortable acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Fredricks' plans derailed by pushy kids.

jokes.text: THE 4 MEN ARE GONNA HAVE SOME FUN.
jokes.setup: Group outing now includes all the geek friends
jokes.punchline: Fredricks' attempt to make it sound manly
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Fredricks trying to salvage masculine bonding with nerdy kids.

jokes.text: IF I WAS SUCH A PRUDE, YOU WOULDN'T EXIST.
jokes.punchline: Dad's sexual comeback
jokes.explanation: Good dad joke. Perfect economy and unexpected sexual reference from conservative character.

jokes.text: YOU JUST KEEP THOSE BOYS AWAY FROM YOUR ACCORDION.
jokes.setup: Dad finally allows Lindsay to go to concert
jokes.punchline: References 'squeeze box' euphemism from song
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and euphemism. Dad shows he understood the innuendo while maintaining parental authority.

jokes.text: HEY, NEAL, I GOT YOU A PRESENT.
jokes.setup: Sam pretending to give Neal money
jokes.punchline: It's fake dollar that snaps back
jokes.explanation: Standard prank gag. Shows the childish dynamic but nothing special.

jokes.text: HEY, NEAL, I GOT YOU A PRESENT. ISN'T THAT GREAT?
jokes.setup: Fredricks trying to bond with the boys
jokes.punchline: Giving them fake vomit/dog poop from joke shop
jokes.explanation: Fredricks' misguided attempt to connect through juvenile humor

jokes.text: 'LADY L'? YOU SHOULDN'T USE YOUR REAL NAME WHEN YOU'RE WRITING THAT STUFF.
jokes.punchline: Nick's practical songwriting advice
jokes.explanation: Good practical advice that shows Nick's experience with amateur art. Deflates Daniel's pretensions.

jokes.text: LOTS OF WACKOS USE THEIR REAL EMOTIONS.
jokes.setup: Daniel defends using real emotions in art
jokes.punchline: Nick's cynical wisdom about artistic sincerity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Nick wisdom. Cuts through artistic pretensions with harsh truth.

jokes.text: YOU ALWAYS HAVE TO WIN. ALL YOU CARE ABOUT IS WINNING. YOU DON'T CARE AT ALL ABOUT OTHER PEOPLE'S FEELINGS.
jokes.punchline: Bill's emotional breakdown over losing
jokes.explanation: Good emotional moment showing Bill's deeper issues with authority and competition.

jokes.text: I LIKE IT WHEN THE GOOD GIRLS CROSS OVER.
jokes.setup: Stroker commenting on Millie joining the freaks
jokes.punchline: Predatory comment about corrupting good girls
jokes.explanation: Shows the darker side of the freak crowd. Establishes threat that Lindsay senses.

jokes.text: IT'S NOT LIKE I'M GONNA DROP ACID OR HAVE SEX IN THE PARKING LOT.
jokes.setup: Millie arguing with her mother about concert
jokes.punchline: Innocent girl listing specific concert dangers
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Millie's protest reveals she knows exactly what she could be tempted to do.

jokes.text: YOU THINK I'M A STUPID JOCK GYM TEACHER, RIGHT? BING! YOU--YOU'RE PROBABLY RIGHT.
jokes.setup: Fredricks trying to connect with Bill
jokes.punchline: Admits Bill's assessment is correct
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing unexpected self-awareness and humility from Fredricks.

jokes.text: THAT--HE--HE'S A CRACK-UP, RIGHT?
jokes.setup: Ken smashes Daniel's guitar in Pete Townshend tribute
jokes.punchline: Daniel's forced laugh after expensive destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment. Daniel trying to play off expensive disaster as cool rock move.

jokes.text: YOU'RE BUYING ME A NEW GUITAR, MAN.
jokes.punchline: Nick's matter-of-fact demand for replacement
jokes.explanation: Good practical response to Ken's rock star pretensions. Nick cuts through the performance to reality.

jokes.text: I KILLED YOUR DOG!
jokes.setup: Lindsay watching Millie drink beer and act rebellious
jokes.punchline: Blurts out confession at worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic timing. Lindsay's guilt erupts at the moment when keeping the secret would be easiest.

jokes.text: I HEAR THAT THE BATHROOMS SMELL REALLY BAD AND THAT IT'S NOT SAFE TO SIT.
jokes.setup: Lindsay and Millie talking about missing the concert
jokes.punchline: Millie's specific concerns about venue hygiene
jokes.explanation: Good Millie character moment showing her practical concerns about rock venues.

jokes.text: COULD YOU IMAGINE HOLDING IT FOR 4 HOURS?
jokes.punchline: Millie's practical concern about bathroom logistics
jokes.explanation: Millie's innocent focus on practical concert concerns rather than music

jokes.text: IT'S OK. I'LL TELL YOU DURING THE COMMERCIAL.
jokes.punchline: Fredricks' patient offer to explain during break
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending showing the relationship might work. Fredricks shows patience and kindness.

tmdb_overview: Sam discovers that life as Cindy's boyfriend is not nearly as exciting as he had imagined. Ken's girlfriend ,Amy, confesses a shocking secret, putting Ken in an awkward situation. Lindsay struggles with asking Vice President George Bush a question during an assembly.
